title: Update and WTF?!
body:
Finished my (55f, IDC2b, ++-) 16 rounds of chemo two weeks ago and had a new mri and finally met with the surgeon again (3rd time total).  First, I’m so frustrated - my tumor shrinkage is negligible and the pathology report says that I’ve had a “partial response to neoadjuvant chemotherapy”.  I looked it up prior to my appointment and brought it up at my appointment since the online literature says that chemo stimulates cancer growth and the surgeon’s response is that it’s nothing to worry about.  She also mentioned the stage zero cancer in my left breast hadn’t been affected at all by the chemo, but it wasn’t a surprise since chemo didn’t work on stage zero. What? First I’d heard that. I was expecting the stage zero to be completely gone! 

The surgeon said not to worry about it because she’s confident that she can get it all with the lumpectomy. I immediately said I was getting a mastectomy and she said I absolutely could not have a mastectomy due to my diabetes and heart condition. WHAT? This is now the 2nd time she’s flip flopped.  I argued that she said I *could* have a mastectomy the 2nd time we met and she denied it. I’m so angry and disheartened. 

Honestly, my first response was to just ask for another surgeon and opinion but surgery is scheduled for mid-January and hubby just wants it done.  I spoke with my advocate and she said it would add another month or two to my wait if I changed surgeons now, so I’m getting a double lumpectomy and a lymph node removed.  

My original notes indicated a 5 week recovery time but now she says it’s 5 days!! I don’t even know what to believe anymore.   I’m not even sure when radiation starts. 

But, on to the chemo aftermath - most of my symptoms are clearing up without problem.  The rash/sores on my arms and hands are almost gone. I had to shave my chin hairs for the first time in 5 months so my hair is started to grow back - yay!

My nails are pretty gross. I’d say one thumbnail is gonna be completely detached within a week and I’ll most likely lose 7 fingernails and 4 toenails total.  The neuropathy in my hands has lessened but not my feet - I can’t feel a whole lot in them except the toenail pain.  Bone pain has lessened to a dull ache, easily ignored.   
Overall, I’m a puffer fish - my ankles and feet are crazy swollen, as well as my hands and face.  No one seems concerned. 

My “journey” has been mostly annoying and I don’t recommend it to anyone.  Thankfully, I have you guys and that has been a lifesaver! Thank you for the support!
